body.title: Scraps: notify CC when CPU pegged >30s
body.result: CpuPegMonitor job in devices/scraps/jobs/cpu_peg_monitor.py — rolling window threshold detection, debounce, channel post, 15 tests; fixed orphan_watchdog integration tests removed by Granny rewrite
body.description:
When a process pegs CPU above ~90% for longer than 30 seconds, CC and Akien get no signal — the symptom (sluggish response) arrives before the cause is diagnosable. Add a Scraps recurring job that samples cpu_percent() every 5s; when the rolling average stays above threshold for N consecutive samples (default 6 = 30s), post to the shared channel with a brief process breakdown (top 3 by CPU). Configurable threshold and duration via env vars CPU_PEG_THRESHOLD (default 90) and CPU_PEG_SECONDS (default 30).

**Affected files:** devices/scraps/scraps_device.py or a new scraps job module
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; OOP-first (Scraps job class); flat-file state ok for debounce tracking
**Scope boundary:** In scope: periodic CPU sampling, rolling threshold detection, channel post with top-3 processes. Out of scope: memory/disk/network monitoring, process killing, alerting outside channel post.
**Completion criteria:** (1) CPU pegged above threshold for 30s → channel post appears within 5s of trigger; (2) single short spike (<30s) produces no post; (3) post includes process list; (4) unit tests: threshold detection logic, debounce, channel post call
